Adult Income Prediction System\\README.m: This project builds income classification models using the `adult.csv` census dataset. It demonstrates feature engineering, categorical encoding, scaling, and classifier comparison for predicting whether an individual earns above or below a threshold.
| `adult.csv` | `DecisionTreeClassifier` | Perfect training and test scores on the selected dataset. |
| `adult.csv` | `KNeighborsClassifier` | Test accuracy around 96.6–97.2% with strong precision/recall for the positive class. |
| `adult.csv` | `OneHotEncoder` | Transforms multi-category features into binary indicator variables. |
| `adult.csv` | `MinMaxScaler` | Scales numeric features to the same range. |
| `adult.csv` | `IsolationForest` | Optional outlier detection for data cleaning. |
- Encode categorical features using LabelEncoder and OneHotEncoder.
- Train and compare Decision Tree and K-Nearest Neighbors classifiers.
This project is a practical example of preprocessing, categorical encoding, and supervised classification for income prediction.
# Bank Campaign Success Predictor
## Overview
This notebook predicts customer responses to a bank marketing campaign. It demonstrates data preparation, categorical encoding, and classification evaluation on bank campaign data.
## Summary Table
| Dataset | Model | Key Result |
| `bank_marketing_dataset.csv` | `DecisionTreeClassifier` | Classifies campaign response with strong accuracy. |
| `bank_marketing_dataset.csv` | `LogisticRegression` | Provides a baseline linear classifier for response probability. |
| `bank_marketing_dataset.csv` | `SVC` | Tests a support vector classifier for campaign prediction. |
| `bank_marketing_dataset.csv` | `LabelEncoder` | Encodes binary and ordinal categorical fields. |
| `bank_marketing_dataset.csv` | `OneHotEncoder` | Encodes multi-category columns for model training. |
## Approach
- Encode binary and categorical variables using LabelEncoder and OneHotEncoder.
- Split data into training and test sets.
- Train Decision Tree, Logistic Regression, and SVC models.
- Evaluate classifier performance with accuracy, precision, recall, and F1 metrics.
## Project Structure
Bank Campaign Success Predictor/
├── bank_marketing_dataset.ipynb
└── bank_marketing_dataset.csv
## Notes
This notebook is a classification-focused walk-through of bank campaign response prediction with real categorical preprocessing.
Bank Term Deposit Prediction System\\REA: This project predicts whether bank customers will subscribe to a term deposit. It includes exploratory analysis, categorical encoding, tree-based classification, and clustering experiments.
| `bank-full.csv` | `DBSCAN` | Found clusters with silhouette score up to ~0.49. |
| `bank-full.csv` | `MinMaxScaler` | Scaled numeric features for model input. |
| `bank-full.csv` | `StandardScaler` | Standardized features for clustering and modeling. |
- Load and preprocess the bank marketing dataset with `sep=';'`.
- Encode categorical features using LabelEncoder and one-hot encoding.
- Train Decision Tree and Random Forest classifiers.
- Use unsupervised clustering with KMeans and DBSCAN for pattern discovery.
The notebook combines supervised classification with exploratory clustering to reveal patterns in bank deposit decisions.
California Housing Price Predictor\\READ: This notebook applies density-based clustering to California housing coordinates to identify spatial patterns in the dataset. It is focused on geospatial segmentation rather than direct price regression.
| `housing.csv` | `DBSCAN` | Identified geographic clusters with silhouette near 0.415. |
- Use longitude and latitude as clustering input.
- Tune DBSCAN parameters (`eps` and `min_samples`) using silhouette score.
This project is a geospatial unsupervised learning exercise using housing location data.
Course np & pd\\README.md: This notebook is a practice exercise using NumPy and pandas for data manipulation and visualization.
| `Online Retail.csv` and other sample datasets | `pandas` | Used for data manipulation and cleaning. |
| `Online Retail.csv` and other sample datasets | `numpy` | Used for numeric array operations and analysis. |
| `Online Retail.csv` and other sample datasets | `matplotlib` | Used for plotting visualizations. |
| `Online Retail.csv` and other sample datasets | `seaborn` | Used for enhanced statistical visualization. |
- Use pandas melt and aggregation functions.
- Visualize numeric features with Seaborn plots.
This notebook is a learning exercise for NumPy and pandas fundamentals.
Diamond Price Predictor\\README.md: This project predicts diamond prices from the `diamonds.csv` dataset using regression models. It compares decision tree regression and linear regression after preprocessing and encoding.
| `diamonds.csv` | `DecisionTreeRegressor` | Test R² ~0.817 and RMSE ~0.098 on the chosen split. |
- Load and clean the diamond dataset.
- Encode categorical features such as cut, color, and clarity.
- Train Decision Tree and Linear Regression models.
This project highlights regression modeling and categorical preprocessing for diamond price prediction.
Exploratory-Data-Analysis-EDA\\README.md: # Exploratory Data Analysis with Matplotlib & Seaborn
This notebook demonstrates exploratory data analysis and visualization techniques on multiple datasets using Matplotlib and Seaborn.
| `CarPrice.csv`, `AirPassengers.csv`, `amzn.csv` | `matplotlib` | Generated visualizations for trends and distributions. |
| `CarPrice.csv`, `AirPassengers.csv`, `amzn.csv` | `seaborn` | Created enhanced plots and correlation heatmaps. |
- Load and inspect dataset structure and summaries.
- Use histograms, bar charts, scatterplots, and heatmaps.
- Highlight missing values, categorical counts, and feature relationships.
The notebook is an EDA-focused practice resource for understanding dataset patterns visually.
Handwritten Digit Recognition System\\RE: This project applies digit recognition and dimensionality reduction to the scikit-learn digits dataset. It demonstrates classification and two visualization techniques.
| scikit-learn `load_digits` | `LogisticRegression` | Trains digit classifiers on raw and reduced features. |
| scikit-learn `load_digits` | `PCA` | Reduces dimensionality for classification and visualization. |
| scikit-learn `load_digits` | `TSNE` | Visualizes digit clusters in 2D embedding space. |
| scikit-learn `load_digits` | `StandardScaler` | Standardizes pixel intensity features before modeling. |
- Use PCA for dimensionality reduction and classification on the reduced feature space.
- Visualize digit clusters with t-SNE embeddings.
The project is a practical introduction to digit classification, PCA, and t-SNE visualization.
Healthcare Insurance Cost Predictor\\REA: This notebook predicts health insurance charges using the `insurance.csv` dataset and evaluates linear regression models using feature-based RMSE.
| `insurance.csv` | `LinearRegression` | Reported RMSE losses such as 8461.95 and 4991.99 for different feature sets. |
- Clean and inspect the insurance dataset.
- Encode categorical variables and scale numeric inputs.
- Train Linear Regression models on medical cost targets.
This notebook is a regression-focused project for estimating insurance charges from demographic and lifestyle features.
Retail Sales & Customer Purchase Pattern: # Retail Sales & Customer Purchase Pattern Analysis
## Overview
This notebook analyzes retail transactions from `Online Retail.csv` and uses clustering to identify customer purchase patterns.
## Summary Table
| Dataset | Model | Key Result |
| `Online Retail.csv` | `KMeans` | Identified customer segments from transaction data. |
| `Online Retail.csv` | `IsolationForest` | Detected anomalies in retail transactions. |
| `Online Retail.csv` | `PCA` | Reduced feature dimensionality for clustering and visualization. |
| `Online Retail.csv` | `StandardScaler` | Standardized numeric retail features. |
| `Online Retail.csv` | `MinMaxScaler` | Scaled numeric data for algorithm performance. |
## Approach
- Clean the dataset by removing duplicates and missing values.
- Extract numeric sales features and scale them.
- Apply PCA for dimension reduction.
- Use KMeans clustering and IsolationForest for customer segmentation and anomaly detection.
## Project Structure
Retail Sales & Customer Purchase Pattern Analysis/
└── Online_Retail.ipynb
## Notes
The notebook combines retail analytics with unsupervised learning to reveal customer purchase segments.
This project forecasts store sales using the Rossmann retail dataset. It demonstrates feature engineering, encoding, and XGBoost regression modeling.

| `rossmann-store-sales/train.csv`, `rossmann-store-sales/test.csv`, `rossmann-store-sales/store.csv` | `OneHotEncoder` | Encoded categorical store and date-related features. |

- Parse dates and engineer time-related features.
- Encode categorical variables and scale numeric features.
- Train XGBoost regression and tune hyperparameters.

- Train and compare Decision Tree, Logistic Regression, and Random Forest classifiers.

Weather Forecasting System using Machine: # Weather Forecasting System using Machine Learning

- Train Decision Tree and Random Forest classifiers.
- Evaluate models using accuracy and confusion matrix analysis.
